content: node --test glob: shell glob (test/*.test.mjs) is expanded by the shell before node sees it, so test/**/*.test.mjs fails on bash unless quoted. Fix: either use node --test with double-quoted globs so node handles glob expansion internally (node --test '**/*.test.mjs'), or pass multiple explicit globs. Node 22 docs confirm node --test accepts glob patterns as final args and handles them internally — patterns should be double-quoted to prevent shell expansion. The built-in default patterns include **/*.test.{cjs,mjs,js} recursively, so bare node --test (no args) already discovers subdirectories. Current ci.yml uses node --test test/*.test.mjs which shell-expands to only root-level files.
